destinations.name: Darjeeling
destinations.description: The "Queen of the Hills", Darjeeling is classic, historical, and dramatic. Famed for its lush tea production, breathtaking views of the majestic Kanchenjunga range, and the historical Toy Train, it remains a crown jewel of tourism across India's mountain ranges.
destinations.bestSeason: September to June (Clear skies in autumn and spring)

destinations.name: Kalimpong
destinations.description: Perched on a ridge overlooking the Teesta River, Kalimpong is famous for colonial-era architecture, vibrant nurseries, Buddhist monasteries, and a tranquil atmosphere. Located at an altitude of 4,100 ft, it offers perfect moderate weather year-round.

destinations.name: Lava
destinations.description: A cozy, remote forest-bound hamlet at 7,200 ft bordering Neora Valley National Park. Lava is loved for its pine trees, misty pathways, and the sacred Lava Monastery, offering a pristine alpine experience away from commercialized regions.

Surrounded by towering cypress and heritage oak trees, Loleygaon is a tiny serene Lepcha village. The Canopy Walk, a suspension bridge hanging high in the rain forest, lets you walk among ancient tree branches covered in moss.
destinations.name: Loleygaon

destinations.description: Centering around the tranquil Sumendu Lake, Mirik is a beautiful lake retreat surrounded by pine forests and sprawling high-slope tea estates. Perfect for families, boating, and enjoying spectacular views of the snow ranges.
destinations.name: Mirik
Sittong
destinations.description: Recognized as the "Orange Village", Sittong is a collection of cascading hamlets nestled in a valley. Known for its endless terraced orange orchards, local wooden bridges over running mountain creeks, and gorgeous home hospitality.
destinations.bestSeason: November to January (Harvest peak for oranges)

attractions.description: A series of wooden plank suspension bridges tied between giant, ancient conifers, letting nature lovers experience walk-pathways up to 50ft above the forest floor.
attractions.name: Loleygaon Canopy Walk

attractions.description: Offering panoramic vistas of Kalimpong town, the surrounding valley, the Teesta riverbed, and far-reaching Himalayan peaks from its quiet hilltop monastery.
attractions.name: Durpin Dara Hill viewpoint

attractions.description: One of the oldest Tibetan Buddhist monasteries in the region, built in 1850, preserving rare palm-leaf manuscripts and a grand 15-foot statue of Maitreya Buddha.
attractions.name: Yiga Choeling (Ghoom Monastery)

attractions.description: Nestled amidst thick pine forest and heavy mountain mists, this bright brick-red structure is absolute peace personified, home to hundreds of practicing monks.
attractions.name: Lava Jamgyong Kongtrul Monastery
Namthing Pokhari & Jogighat Bridge
attractions.description: A historic suspension bridge over the Riyang river at Jogighat, along with a seasonal mountain lake Namthing Pokhari which is home to the rare Himalayan Salamander.
An idyllic 1.25 km lake reflecting green pine groves and snow ridges, with a gorgeous arched footbridge swinging across the lake waters and options for boat trips.
attractions.name: Sumendu Lake & Arch Bridge
Tiger Hill Sunrise Point
attractions.description: World-famous sunrise lookout situated 11km from Darjeeling. At dawn, watch the sun paint Mt. Kanchenjunga in shades of gold, fire, and salmon pink.
A beautiful Tibetan monastery consecrated by the Dalai Lama in 1976, housing incredibly detailed frescoes, mandalas, and ancient scriptures on the Durpin hill.
attractions.name: Zang Dhok Palri Phodang

images.caption: Admin visual for highland spot
images.altText: Admin published official high resolution photograph of hill sightseeing
